title: cytisus leucanthus subsp. pallidus
intro: Cytisus leucanthus subsp. pallidus, also known as the pale broom or pale Scotch broom, is a flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cytisus leucanthus subsp. pallidus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Cytisus leucanthus subsp. pallidus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
